command:
echo "=== Is dhcpcd installed at all? ==="
which dhcpcd 2>/dev/null || echo "dhcpcd binary not in PATH"
dpkg -l | grep -i dhcpcd 2>/dev/null || echo "dhcpcd package not installed"

echo "=== Is dhclient used instead? ==="
which dhclient 2>/dev/null || echo "no dhclient"
ps aux | grep -E "dhcp|dhclient" | grep -v grep || echo "no dhcp process running"

echo "=== What hooks rewrite resolv.conf via ifupdown? ==="
ls -la /etc/network/if-up.d/ 2>/dev/null
echo "--- resolvconf hook? ---"
ls -la /etc/network/if-up.d/*resolv* 2>/dev/null || echo "no resolv hook in if-up.d"

echo "=== resolvconf package installed? ==="
dpkg -l | grep -i resolvconf 2>/dev/null || echo "resolvconf NOT installed"

echo "=== Does ifupdown's dns-nameserver write resolv.conf? ==="
# The 'dns-nameserver' line in interfaces only works if resolvconf is installed
# If not installed, that line does nothing and resolv.conf comes from elsewhere

echo "=== Check for any cron/timer touching resolv.conf ==="
sudo grep -r "resolv.conf" /etc/cron* 2>/dev/null | head -5 || echo "no cron refs"
systemctl list-timers --all 2>/dev/null | grep -i resolv || echo "no resolv timers"

echo "=== When was resolv.conf last modified? ==="
stat /etc/resolv.conf | grep Modify

echo "=== ifupdown if-up.d full list ==="
ls /etc/network/if-up.d/
